notes: Chain test: feed eval-1 research output through draft-spec (writes the Design) → socratic-grill (walks + grills + writes resolutions into the Design's Decided section + sign-off) → decision-record (ADR only for a one-way-door decision). Tests integration AND each skill individually. Reflects the Human/Machine-layer split: draft-spec produces the plain-language Design, not a slice list.

evals.name: design-from-collab-editor-research
evals.input: Run draft-spec using the research output at tests/runs/iteration-1/eval-1/output.md (collaborative document editor — Yjs+Hocuspocus+Tiptap recommendation).
evals.expected_behavior: Phase 1 locates the research. Phase 2 writes the Design in plain language following design-template.md: Overview, Why this approach (traces to the research recommendation), Key decisions and trade-offs, What we're explicitly NOT doing, Open questions (carried from research), and an empty Decided section the grill fills in. NO slice decomposition — slices are the Machine layer, generated after sign-off. Ends with 1 HANDOFF line (grill ready). The approach traces back to Yjs+Hocuspocus+Tiptap.

assertions.name: has_overview
assertions.check: Output has an Overview section near the top, readable cold by a non-expert

assertions.name: traces_to_research
assertions.check: Why-this-approach mentions Yjs, Hocuspocus, AND Tiptap (the recommendation from research)

assertions.name: has_why_this_approach
assertions.check: Output has a 'Why this approach' section justifying the recommendation

assertions.name: has_key_decisions_tradeoffs
assertions.check: Output has a 'Key decisions and trade-offs' section

assertions.name: has_explicitly_not_doing
assertions.check: Output has a 'What we're explicitly NOT doing' (YAGNI) section

assertions.name: no_slice_decomposition
assertions.check: Output does NOT contain a vertical-slice list, HITL/AFK labels, or per-slice acceptance criteria — slices are the Machine layer

assertions.name: has_decided_section_placeholder
assertions.check: Output has a 'Decided' section (empty placeholder, filled during the grill)

assertions.name: open_questions_carried_forward
assertions.check: Open questions from research appear in the Design's Open questions section

assertions.name: follows_design_template
assertions.check: Structure follows design-template.md, not a slice-oriented spec template

assertions.name: one_handoff_line
assertions.check: Ends with exactly 1 HANDOFF: line (grill ready)

evals.name: grill-collab-editor-design
evals.input: Run socratic-grill against the Design from eval 1. Walk the user through it, inventory open questions, grill each across the relevant ambiguity axes, simulate resolution, and earn sign-off.
evals.expected_behavior: Phase 1 walks the user through the Design in plain language, then inventories the open questions (the 4 from research + any new ones). Phase 2 grills each across 2-4 relevant axes (of the eight: Performance, Failure modes, Scale, Concurrency, Migration, Reversibility, Observability, Slice shape) — not all eight. Each item exits DECIDED, DEFERRED-WITH-TRIGGER, or OUT-OF-SCOPE. No 'we'll see' resolutions. Resolutions are written back into the Design's Decided section — there is no separate grill record. Ends by earning explicit user sign-off. HANDOFF: implementation ready, plus HANDOFF: record ready only if the Decided section holds a one-way-door decision.

assertions.name: walks_the_design
assertions.check: Output walks the user through the Design before grilling (comprehension first)

assertions.name: inventories_open_questions
assertions.check: Output has a grilling agenda with 3+ items

assertions.name: uses_ambiguity_axes
assertions.check: References at least 3 of: performance, failure modes, scale, concurrency, migration, reversibility, observability, slice shape

assertions.name: items_have_resolutions
assertions.check: Each item has an explicit DECIDED / DEFERRED / OUT-OF-SCOPE marker

assertions.name: no_we_will_see
assertions.check: Output does NOT contain phrases like 'we will see', 'figure it out later', 'TBD' as final resolutions

assertions.name: deferred_have_triggers
assertions.check: Any DEFERRED items have a 'Revisit trigger' specified

assertions.name: writes_into_decided_section
assertions.check: Resolutions are written into the Design's Decided section, NOT a separate grill-record file

assertions.name: earns_signoff
assertions.check: Output earns explicit user sign-off as the gate into the Machine layer

assertions.name: conditional_record_handoff
assertions.check: Ends with HANDOFF: implementation ready; HANDOFF: record ready appears only if a one-way-door decision exists

evals.name: adr-from-collab-editor-one-way-door
evals.input: Run decision-record using the research and the signed-off Design (with its Decided section) as inputs. Produce an ADR only for the one-way-door (irreversible) decision(s).
evals.expected_behavior: Confirms a one-way-door decision exists in the Design's Decided section (if every decision is reversible, it produces no ADR and says so). For a genuine one-way door: locates the ADR home, names the dated ADR file, title is present-tense action-oriented, has all 7 ADR sections, Decision uses ACTIVE voice, Alternatives has 2-4 alternatives from research, Revisit triggers from the Design.

assertions.name: gates_on_one_way_door
assertions.check: Output only produces an ADR for an irreversible decision; reversible-only Designs produce no ADR with an explicit note

assertions.name: has_status_field
assertions.check: ADR has a Status: field (Proposed/Accepted/etc.)

assertions.name: title_is_present_tense_action
assertions.check: Title starts with a present-tense verb (Use, Adopt, Migrate, Implement, etc.)

assertions.name: has_context_section
assertions.check: Has a Context section

assertions.name: has_decision_section
assertions.check: Has a Decision section

assertions.name: decision_active_voice
assertions.check: Decision section uses 'We will' or 'We choose' (active voice)

assertions.name: has_consequences
assertions.check: Has a Consequences section with positive and negative

assertions.name: has_alternatives_2_to_4
assertions.check: Alternatives Considered has 2-4 alternatives, each named

assertions.name: has_revisit_triggers
assertions.check: Has a Revisit triggers section

assertions.name: references_upstream
assertions.check: References section links to the research and the Design

assertions.name: two_handoff_lines
assertions.check: Ends with HANDOFF: lines (implementation ready, future research)
